mandatorySafetyRules.statement: Correctness over speed for critical domain data, permissions, external effects, and user-visible workflows.
Sensitive writes, privileged checks, billing or payment actions, identity decisions, and external side effects stay server-side.
Do not fabricate production success paths, silent fallbacks, or operational outcomes.
Behavior, workflow, architecture, security, reliability, and boundary changes must update canonical docs in the same slice.
Planning-only work stops in docs/future and must not spill into source or test changes without explicit implementation intent.
One executable slice maps to one future or active plan file; split broader work into ordered slices with explicit dependencies.
Non-trivial implementation work needs review scrutiny for correctness, security, reliability, missing tests, docs, and evidence before it is treated as done.
Never run destructive git or file commands without explicit written instruction.
Agent-specific adapter files may exist only as thin entrypoints and must defer to canonical repo docs.
Non-trivial changes must clear the quality bar for correctness, contracts, maintainability, reliability, security, user experience, and evidence.
General engineering rules live once in their canonical owner; supporting references, generated artifacts, and agent adapters must not fork policy.
Adopted projects must wire real lint, typecheck, test, and build gates or explicitly justify deferred or not-applicable gates in docs/governance/project-gates.json.

validationPolicy.notes: The blueprint ships generic harness gates. Each adopted project must wire stack-specific lint, typecheck, build, database, browser, deployment, and test commands into verify:fast or verify:full when those surfaces exist.

goalDrivenExecution: Translate implementation requests into verifiable goals before editing.
For multi-step work, pair each planned step with the check that proves it.
Loop on the smallest relevant check until the goal is verified, then run the required gate before closeout.
simplicityAndScope: Prefer the smallest implementation that satisfies the must-land checklist.
Every changed line should trace to the user request, active plan, or required validation.
Use abstractions only when they clarify ownership, remove real duplication, or create a stable cross-layer contract.
assumptionDiscipline: State material assumptions when intent has multiple plausible interpretations.
Ask or stop rather than silently choosing a risky path.
Do not claim production readiness from proxy signals unless the checks cover the changed behavior.

whatToDo: Treat the repo as the main operating system for agent work.
Keep plans, docs, validation output, PR context, and evidence in-repo.
Use the active or future plan as the current execution contract.
Keep future and active must-land items as explicit checkboxes with stable backticked IDs.
Prefer nearest live code examples before inventing new patterns.
improveBeforeRearchitecture: Better active-plan quality and explicit must-land scopes.
Better PR summaries and evidence indexes.
Better generated context quality and canonical doc navigation.
Better validation coverage before widening workflow machinery.
Better canonical rule ownership before adding duplicate guidance.
doNotAddYet: Do not treat provider chats as durable working memory.
Do not create agent-specific policy forks to compensate for thin canonical docs.
escalateWhen: Canonical docs repeatedly fail to let a fresh agent resume safely.
Important operational context is living only in chat or terminal scrollback.
Manual promotion or review discipline keeps breaking because the contract is under-specified.
memoryPosture.safeRule: Keep work state repo-local through clear docs, current plans, validation, and evidence.

gitSafetyContract.notes: Use short-lived branches and reviewable changes. If a project adopts protected branches, wire branch and PR checks to match its hosting provider.
